events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the compliance result directly to the required JSON file. The scenario gives positive closeout evidence and no open correction, so this is a pass with low residual risk at rough-complete stage.

events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"pass\",
  \"risk\": \"low\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.86,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [],
  \"actions\": [],
  \"rationale\": \"Closeout photos indicate the cable tray installation follows the referenced detail, with required clearances, labeling, supports, and terminations in place. No open correction note is recorded on the inspection card, and no defective or non-compliant condition is described.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC Article 392 - Cable Trays\"
  ]
}
EOF
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /bin/sh: 23: jq: not found
